channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: New York budget likely to be late again as disputes continue
item.description: New York's Legislature is expected to miss its budget deadline once again, as negotiations over housing and education funding remain open.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man sues GOP Rep. Burchett over Super Bowl rally shooting, illegal immigrant claims
item.description: Denton Loudermill Jr. is suing Republican Tennessee Rep. Tim Burchett for allegedly falsely accusing him of being one of the Kansas City Chiefs Super Bowl rally shooters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kansas lawmakers advance crackdown on Chinese land buys, economic activity
item.description: Kansas lawmakers advanced proposals Wednesday to curb economic activity by Chinese entities, including farmland and business property purchases.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tennessee bill authorizing use of death penalty for child rapists moves 1 step closer to becoming law
item.description: A Tennessee bill that would authorize the use of the death penalty for convicted child rapists has passed its first legislative hurdle as the Senate Judiciary Committee has moved the bill forward.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Los Angeles Deputy Mayor Raymond Chan convicted in sprawling bribery case
item.description: Raymond Chan, former Deputy Mayor of Los Angeles, has been convicted of fraud, bribery, racketeering and for 'giving false statements to investigators' in a corruption scandal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden leans on his Democratic predecessors as Trump remains isolated from other Republican leaders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — When President Joe Biden needs advice, there are two people he can turn to who know what it’s like to sit in his chair. Sometimes he will invite Barack Obama over to the White House for a meal or he will get on the phone with Bill Clinton.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cash-strapped Trump is now selling $60 Bibles, U.S. Constitution included
item.description: The “God Bless The USA” Bible is inspired by the Lee Greenwood anthem and includes copies of the nation’s founding documents. Trump launched it ahead of Easter as his financial woes mount.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: Talks resume on bringing Israeli officials to the US to discuss Gaza operation, the White House says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Talks have restarted aimed at bringing top Israeli officials to Washington to discuss potential military operations in Gaza, after Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu canceled a planned visit this week because he was angry about the U.S. vote on a U.N.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and SEUNG MIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hurry up and wait: Trump's classified documents case is mired in delays that may run past election
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The case against Donald Trump seemed relatively straightforward in August 2022 when FBI agents searched his Mar-a-Lago estate, with authorities citing evidence that the former president hoarded enough classified documents to fill dozens of boxes and obstructed the government's effo
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Should college essays touch on race? Some feel the affirmative action ruling leaves them no choice
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — When she started writing her college essay, Hillary Amofa told the story she thought admissions offices wanted to hear. About being the daughter of immigrants from Ghana and growing up in a small apartment in Chicago. About hardship and struggle. Then she deleted it all.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, ANNIE MA and NOREEN NASIR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump agitates hush money judge as he seeks to stave off NY trial
item.description: Former President Trump is pulling out all the stops to stave off the start of his first criminal trial, but the New York judge overseeing the case is increasingly appearing to lose his patience in the matter.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DeSantis signs bill to increase penalties for squatters, protect homeowners in Florida
item.description: Governor Ron DeSantis signed House Bill 621 in Orlando on Wednesday, ending the squatters scam in the state of Florida.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Minnesota electric vehicle rebates coming to qualified car buyers starting in April
item.description: Most of the money in a Minnesota electric vehicle rebate program has been claimed and checks will start going out in late April.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Settlement reached in lawsuit between Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis allies and Disney
item.description: Allies of Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis and Disney reached a settlement agreement Wednesday in a lawsuit over who controls Walt Disney World’s governing district.
item.dc:creator: Mike Schneider, WFLA, NBC

item.title: MTA expected to vote on final congestion pricing rates at board meeting
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – It’s been a long time coming and has faced a lot of pushback, but we may have a final answer on the MTA’s controversial congestion pricing plan on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Kiran Dhillon, PIX 11

item.title: Democrats ramp up attacks on RFK Jr.: 'He should be ashamed of himself'
item.description: Democrats are stepping up their criticism of Robert F. Kennedy Jr. after he announced his pick of Nicole Shanahan for vice president Tuesday, arguing the duo will benefit former President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, NewsNation

item.title: Democrat wins Alabama special election after focusing on IVF, abortion
item.description: Democratic candidate Marilyn Lands has won the special election in Alabama state House District 10, according to a projection from Decision Desk HQ. Lands, a licensed professional counselor, beat Republican Teddy Powell, a Madison City Council member, to fill the seat.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Trump seeks to capitalize on Biden’s frustration with Israel’s Netanyahu
item.description: Support for Israel's war in Gaza has fractured along political and religious lines. But Trump's own remarks about Jewish Americans have been sharply criticized.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez, MPR News

item.title: DNC 'running incredible assault on democracy': RFK Jr. donor
item.description: Independent presidential candidate Robert F. Kennedy Jr. announced Nicole Shanahan as his running mate.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Trump is selling 'God Bless USA' Bibles
item.description: The $60 Bible will include a copy of Greenwood’s “God Bless the U.S.A.,” as well as the U.S. Constitution, the Bill of Rights, the Declaration of Independence and the Pledge of Allegiance.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Bring Chicago Home backers retreat and regroup
item.description: CHICAGO — Days after Chicago voters said no to raising the real estate transfer tax on property sales over $1 million to combat homelessness in the city, supporters of the measure struck a tone of humility.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: NBC News parts with Ronna McDaniel following internal backlash
item.description: NBCU News Group Chair Cesar Conde announced the decision to part with Daniels, who just made her inaugural network appearance Sunday, to staff in a Tuesday memo obtained by The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Instead of peeing in Cheerios, Duluth campaign suggests donating them
item.description: Duluth residents have turned a recent viral comment from billionaire Kathy Cargill on its head, instead using it to motivate people to donate boxes of cereal to families in need.
item.dc:creator: Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: Legislation would break out $45 million for Hoover Dam locked up by red tape
item.description: When Hoover Dam was built in the 1930s, it cost $49 million. Now, congressional leaders are mounting an effort to "free up" $45 million in the Colorado River Dam fund that has been "inaccessible for decades due to bureaucracy and federal red tape.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge overseeing NY hush money trial imposes gag order on Trump
item.description: The New York judge overseeing former President Trump’s hush money trial imposed a gag order on Trump on Tuesday, preventing him from publicly attacking witnesses, jurors and others during what is slated to be the first-ever criminal trial of a former U.S. president.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Senate GOP cautions Trump on 15-week national abortion ban
item.description: Senate Republicans are warning former President Trump that restrictions on abortion should be left to the states and that proposing a 15-week national abortion ban ahead of this year’s election would be a major political blunder.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Alabama special election to test political potency of abortion, IVF
item.description: A special election in an unusual purple Alabama state House district is testing the political potency of reproductive rights in a red state after a major ruling there last month led to a temporary pause on in vitro fertilization (IVF) treatment.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, Fox 5

item.title: In Texas, ex-oil and gas workers champion geothermal energy as a replacement for fossil-fueled power plants
item.description: Texas has become an early hot spot for geothermal energy exploration as scores of former oil industry workers and executives are taking their knowledge to a new energy source.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, Emily Foxhall, KPRC 2

item.title: Court agrees to pause collection of Trump's massive civil fraud judgment if he puts up $175M
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A New York appeals court on Monday agreed to hold off collection of former President Donald Trump’s more than $454 million civil fraud judgment if he puts up $175 million within 10 days.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Can a candidate be replaced after winning the presidential primary?
item.description: Both presumptive candidates for the presidential election in 2024 will be at least 78 years old or older by Inauguration Day 2025. One is also charged in four criminal cases and facing 88 felony counts, which could lead the party to shift tactics pending a conviction.
item.dc:creator: Justin Kounelias, Fox 5

item.title: Mayor Adams meets alleged dealers at 'drug bazaar' Burger King: reports
item.description: MANHATTAN, N.Y. (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams met with alleged drug dealers at a Burger King described as a "drug bazaar" near City Hall, according to the New York Post. Adams stopped by the fast food location to assess the situation himself following a complaint, according to a City Hall spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Texas energy companies are betting hydrogen can become a cleaner fuel for transportation
item.description: Supporters say developing hydrogen as a fuel is critical to slowing climate change. Critics are concerned that producing it with fossil fuels will prop up the oil and gas industry.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, Emily Foxhall, KPRC 2

item.title: NJ first lady Tammy Murphy suspends her Democratic campaign for US Senate
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — New Jersey first lady Tammy Murphy has suspended her U.S. Senate campaign to replace Democratic Sen. Robert Menendez as he faces federal corruption charges.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump spirals as bond deadline in fraud case nears
item.description: The eye-popping judgment against former President Trump in his New York fraud case has laid bare his precarious financial status, a matter that has sent the notoriously incensed real estate mogul off the rails.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mail-in ballots still being counted in razor-tight primary race for Cook County State's Attorney
item.description: CHICAGO — The razor-tight Democratic primary race for Cook County's top prosecutor is coming down to ballots counted in a basement.
item.dc:creator: Sean Lewis, WGN 9

item.title: Nevada Secretary of State fires back, asks for more funding from Congress
item.description: Nevada Secretary of State Francisco Aguilar responded on Friday to a Republican congressman's request for an explanation why voters' history records showed they had voted in the presidential preference primary even though they had not cast a ballot.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: California voters pass proposition requiring counties to spend on programs to tackle homelessness
item.description: California voters have passed a measure that will impose strict requirements on counties to spend on housing and drug treatment programs to tackle the state’s homelessness crisis.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5
